page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, resulting in 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is weak, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%, alongside poor single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance is average, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression all scoring in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 654.94 GB/sec for small cached blocks and approximately 69.78 GB/sec for large uncached reads.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is its reliance on external storage and its lower single-threaded efficiency. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation provides stable, non-burstable compute resources suitable for workloads that can leverage parallel execution. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, but less optimal for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and static web serving performing in the middle tier. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe drives or high single-threaded performance. It is suited for general-purpose multi-threaded applications, web serving, and standard database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 7 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core performance on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core virtual server with balanced memory allocation, optimized for parallelized workloads and standard web serving without local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
7 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external data transfer
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database operations
